memoryArtifacts.headline: Hugh Laskin (1587 – 21 March 1658)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Born by about 1587 (based on estimated date of marriage). Planter who came to Massachusetts Bay in 1635 & settled in Salem. Died by 21 March 1658/9 (date of inventory).

Married by about 1612 Alice __ …

zh.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskin於1587年之前出生在英國多瑟特布洛阿德文德索。他於1610年與Alis在英國萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1635年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼薩萊姆被列為planter in salem, massachusetts。他去世於1659年3月21日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskin于1587年之前出生在英国多瑟特布洛阿德文德索。他于1610年与Alis在英国萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1635年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼薩萊姆被列为planter in salem, massachusetts。他去世于1659年3月21日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆。

nameMeanings.name: Laskin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Nochum, Yitzchak, Yoav, Yochanan.
Russian and Belorussian: nickname from Russian and Belorussian laska ‘favor’, also ‘weasel’ + the possessive suffix -in.
Jewish (from Belarus): either of the same origin as 1 above, or from the personal name Laske, a pet form of the Biblical name Elazar (see Lazar ).

variants.name: Lazar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Moshe, Yonah, Amnon, Chaim, Ilanit, Naama, Naum, Shimon, Shira.

Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, German, Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian; Polish (also Łazar); Slovak (also Lazár and Lázár); Hungarian (Lázár); Romanian (Lazăr): from a Biblical personal name of Aramaic origin, a shortened form of the Hebrew name Elazar, composed of the elements El ‘God’ + azar ‘help’, and meaning ‘may God help him’ or ‘God has helped (i.e., by granting a son)’. This was established in central Europe as a Jewish name, but was also popular among Christians because it is recorded in the New Testament. Lazaros is the New Testament Greek form of the name of the brother of Martha and Mary, who was restored to life by Christ (John 11: 1–44). As a non-Jewish name it was also used as a nickname for a beggar or an outcast leper; this use arises from the parable of Dives and Lazarus (Luke 16: 19–31). In North America, this surname has also absorbed various other European cognates and their derivatives, e.g. Albanian Lazri, Lazëri, and Llazari (from the personal names Lazër and Llazar; see Lazer ), Greek patronymics like Lazarakis, Lazaridis , and Lazaropoulos, Serbian patronymic Lazarević (see Lazarevic ).

Slovenian: from lazar, a topographic name for someone who lived in an area of cleared woodland, itself derived from laz ‘cleared woodland’. Compare Austrian German and Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) Lasser 3 and Loser 3, and also Lazzari 2.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskin was born before 1587, in Broadwindsor, Dorset, England. He married Alis in 1610, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. In 1635, his occupation is listed as planter in salem, massachusetts in Salem, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. He died before 21 March 1659, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskinは1587年以前にイギリスドールセトブロアドウィンドソルで生まれた。1610年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスターでAlisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1635年，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーサレムでの職業はplanter in salem, massachusettsと記録されている。彼は1659年3月21日以前にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサレムで死去した。

ko.Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘
ko.Salem, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 살렘
ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskin(은)는 1587년 이전 영국 도르세트 브로드윈드소르에서 태어났다. 그는 1610년 영국 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 Alis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1635년, 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 살렘에서 planter in salem, massachusetts이었다. 그는 1659년 3월 21일 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskin nasceu antes 1587, em Broadwindsor, Dorset, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Alis em 1610, em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Em 1635, sua profissão aparece como planter in salem, massachusetts em Salem, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu antes 21 de março de 1659, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Laskin родился перед 1587 г. в местности броадвиндзор, дорсет, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Alis в 1610 г. в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. В 1635 году он работал по профессии planter in salem, massachusetts в местности сеилем, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер перед 21 марта 1659 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
